summary: The CI job failed because a `wget` download of the nydus static binary from GitHub releases returned an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway error, causing the Docker buildx bake process to fail. This is a transient network/infrastructure issue unrelated to the code being tested.
root_cause: The build step that downloads the nydus binary via wget (`wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz`) received an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway response, causing the process to fail and the entire buildx bake to abort.

evidence.quote: wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: The wget command received a 502 error from the server, indicating a transient network/proxy failure when downloading the nydus binary.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$N…
evidence.reason: This confirms the buildx bake failure was directly caused by the wget process failing to complete successfully.
The Docker build step #72 explicitly errored out due to the failed wget download.

evidence.quote: time="2026-05-06T19:55:11Z" level=debug msg="fetch failed" digest="sha256:0288b5a0d7e71a2cdbdf114cb9869ce2bc4b05adfd6aa5e3ee9fa4b3847e7b80" error="failed to do request: Ge…
evidence.reason: Additional fetch failures around the same time corroborate a broader network connectivity issue during the build.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI job, as this is a transient 502 Bad Gateway error from the GitHub releases server. If the issue persists, consider adding retry logic to the wget command (e.g., `wget --tries=3`) or mirroring the nydus binary to a more reliable location.
